records.ety: [F. dner, OF. disner, LL. disnare, contr. fr. an assumed disjunare; dis- + an assumed junare (OF. juner) to fast, for L. jejunare, fr. jejunus fasting. See Jejune, and cf. Dinner, Djeuner.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dined (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Dining.]

defs: To eat the principal regular meal of the day; to take dinner.
quotes: Now can I break my fast, dine, sup, and sleep.

defs: To give a dinner to; to furnish with the chief meal; to feed; as, to dine a hundred men.
quotes: A table massive enough to have dined Johnnie Armstrong and his merry men.

defs: To dine upon; to have to eat.
